title: Pact with Lilith
wikitext:
[]: Pact with Lilith             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Nov 19, 2008 8:34 pm (I'm not sure if this has been brought up before, but if it has, please feel free to lock this thread.) I was watching my recently acquired Australian DVD release of Rebuild today and came across this interesting line between Seele and Gendo, spoken by one of the monoliths: "Defeating the angels is one small part of our pact with Lilith". This pact is again mentioned in the last scene with Kaworu and Seele: "The time of the pact draws near". Now, in the 1.01 fan sub that I have, the line goes; "To exterminate the angels is but one step in fulfilling our contract with Lilith" Is this talk of pacts and contracts just refering to the Instrumentality project, or could it be something else?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Nov 19, 2008 8:43 pm My guess is it could deal with Instrumentality. Though it could have to do with something else. (While that something else could have to do with a potential sequel, it really is too early to tell.)

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Nov 19, 2008 10:20 pm There's nowhere near enough evidence to tell at this time. Speculation is pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Thu Nov 20, 2008 1:33 am Its not pointless, ofcourse the conclusions we come to are pointless, but, it does fill in time until the next release.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Thu Nov 20, 2008 7:41 am I assume this Pact is the thing about that the children of one Seef of Life are going to be destroyed for the sake of the other seed of life. Something like that would fit in i think. That's why Rei (at least in the german version) said that she is "bound" to the eva pilot thing, in german gramma it fits the idea that she is aware of the pact and is a part of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Nov 20, 2008 1:07 pm Rei was saying in the original version of that scene that she was bound. It was an early series hint at her connection to Lilith (the mother of humanity, which is why Rei would be bound to "everyone".)

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 20, 2008 2:00 pm It might actually be a biblical reference. The Lilim are bound to slay the Children of Adam because of Lilith's and Adam's falling out.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Feb 03, 2014 10:06 pm Woo, ! Xard wrote the following last year in the 3.0 Film Reaction thread: Xard wrote: What I personally noticed during marathon most strongly was the portrayal of SEELE. I had initially thought lack of Instrumentality Committee in 1.0 and 2.0 was done for sake of consistence and as such a change that was not important. Similarly all the talk about "Covenant with Lilith" and other vague mumbojumbo in 1.0 and 2.0 which seemed to imply very different settei for SEELE and VERY ancient history for order's mission I just dismissed as random metaphysical/technobabble lolanno dialogue one shouldn't pay too much attention to. After seeing Q for second time all I can say is I was really, really fucking wrong about all of this.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 04, 2014 9:30 am The contract with Lilith was plainly not fulfiled because there was the 12th Angel in 3.0 (and the unseen 11th Angel), which meant that there were Angels still alive at the time Eva-01 started Pseudo-Evolving. That means that Gendo planned to mess things up by starting 3I with the Angels still alive, which knocked Lilith out of Seele's plans and left her in that messed-up state fused to Eva-06 and the 12th Angel. Eva-13 very nearly made things go Seele's way with 4I, but with Kaworu eliminated, Gendo was again in a position of strength over Seele (hence the line "This will do for now". ) What cards Gendo has remaining with him is a good question, but he seems quite willing to unstick Seele's "Covenant with Lilith" so that he can have his own way.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Feb 04, 2014 9:38 am ^ Yeah, it's something that we seem to overlook due to only seeing them in holographic conferences in 1.0 and 2.0 and apparently badly damaged in 3.0, but SEELE in the Rebuild were much more powerful and influential than their NGE counterpart : while in NGE they were at most a very powerful death cult full of crazy old men jaded by life and existing since a few centuries at most(if the manga canon can apply to the series), here they're a super-Illuminati group made of immortal beings behind almost all of mankind's history and implied to be in touch with a true godlike being, Lilith. My personal theory (fanfiction) is that this famous covenant was made millenia ago, when Lilith somehow entered in contact with what would become the seven monoliths(maybe seven especially intelligent and influential shamans from tribes from all around the world?) and gave them a vision of the ultimate evolution of mankind via Instrumentality, and gave them the mission to guide the flock of mankind toward civilization and technological advance to a point were the Lilin will be able to fight, defeat and use ADAM's offspring to accomplish the ritual of artificial evolution that would make mankind ascend to the next stage of evolution. And to help them in this task, she gave them the secret of immortality(soul uploading in the monoliths) and instructions about the Angels and a scenario to reach their ultimate goal, via the SDSS. Of course, that would mean that not only Lilith was an active player instead of just chilling on her cross, but also that she's on SEELE's side, something that would be problematic if it's revealed that Rei contains Lilith's soul like in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 04, 2014 2:42 pm Thanks for the replies! UrsusArctos wrote: What cards Gendo has remaining with him is a good question, but he seems quite willing to unstick Seele's "Covenant with Lilith" so that he can have his own way.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Feb 04, 2014 3:41 pm Lilith being on SEELE's side doesn't pose a problem. It's not like rebelling against her creators/past self/etc and becoming her own person isn't her main character arc in every Evaverse ever.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 04, 2014 3:44 pm AuraTwilight wrote: It's not like rebelling against her creators/past self/etc and becoming her own person isn't her main character arc in every Evaverse ever.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Feb 04, 2014 7:10 pm Well, it's possible that as Lilith, she pragmatically simply followed a practical path for her progeny to dominate the planet against ADAM's offspring while being removed from their condition due to being an Alien Space Goddess and all, and that her time in a Lilin body as Rei Ayanami made her connect with her children and reconsider her opinion about mankind's future.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Feb 04, 2014 8:10 pm ElMariachi wrote: ADAM's offspring

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Wed Feb 05, 2014 9:36 am Interesting insight about a pact. But... Lilith's dead, so how is the pact still valid? Why are they still following a pact made with a corpse?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Wed Feb 05, 2014 9:45 am Well, if Lilith's soul is still available (either through Rei II or Rei Q) possibly they could try and fulfill the pact using Lilith's soul rather than her physical body.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Feb 05, 2014 9:52 am It's also possible that SEELE perpetrated Lilith's will after her death during the time skip, after all they spent millenia to carry out the HIP, why stop now that they are so close to succeed. Notices that unlike in 1.0 and 2.0, the Covenant with Lilith is never brought by either Gendo, Fuyutsuki or SEELE, they only refer to the "scenario as written in the SDSS". In a sense, the SDSS became Lilith's testament.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Feb 05, 2014 4:23 pm Charsi wrote: Interesting insight about a pact. But... Lilith's dead, so how is the pact still valid? Why are they still following a pact made with a corpse?

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Thu May 08, 2014 10:56 pm The covenant might specify Lilith as being alive for one of the requirements in order for it to be fulfilled. Or, in another direction, there might be some DSS documentation that specifies Lilith to die before later angels to reach a particular outcome. In any event, I personally can't see a covenant with Lilith being fulfilled if Lilith is dead. It's interesting to note how SEELE was "turned off" after Lilith went pop with the removal of the spears and not before, and yet the SEELE members did not seem in the least bit angry or embarrassed, despite the fact that Lilith just blew up. They were surprisingly relaxed about the fact that Gendou just murdered the first angel before the other angels were killed.

I was watching my recently acquired Australian DVD release of Rebuild today and came across this interesting line between Seele and Gendo, spoken by one of the monoliths: "Defeating the angels is one small part of our pact with Lilith&quot
